<p align="center">

Auto-generates Dockerfiles  ·  Builds Compose stacks  ·  Wires
Cloudflare tunnels<br> Provisions Hetzner VPS  ·  Ships with rsync  · 
All from Python
</p>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Install

``` sh
pip install fastops
```

Requires Python 3.10+, a `docker` (or `podman`) CLI on your PATH. For
the full deploy pipeline, you’ll also need `hcloud` CLI,
[Multipass](https://multipass.run), and a `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`.

## Ship — Deploy in One Line

Point
[`launch()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch) at
your app directory and a domain. That’s it.

``` python
from fastops import launch, secrets

envs = secrets('DATABASE_URL', 'JWT_SECRET', 'S3_BUCKET')
ip = launch('./myapp', 'app.example.com', envs=envs)
```

Not ready for a real server yet? Test the identical stack locally first:

### Test locally with [`launch_local`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch_local)

[`launch_local()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch_local)
runs the exact same stack inside a [Multipass](https://multipass.run) VM
on your machine — no cloud account, no cost, no surprises before
go-live.

``` python
from fastops import launch_local

# Spin up a local Ubuntu VM with the full stack
ip = launch_local(src, 'myapp.example.com', envs=envs)

# With extra options — same kwargs work on launch() too
ip = launch_local(
    src,
    'myapp.example.com',
    envs=envs,
    app_volumes={'./data': '/app/data', './static': '/app/static'},
    extra_dirs=['static', 'onnx-community'],
    pkgs=['rclone', 'libsqlite3-dev'],   # forwarded to Dockerfile generation
    crowdsec=True,                        # add CrowdSec intrusion detection
)
```

The Cloudflare tunnel dials **outbound** from inside the VM — no ports
need to be open, and `myapp.example.com` resolves publicly even from
localhost.

### Deploy to production with [`launch`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch)

Swap
[`launch_local`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch_local)
for [`launch`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch) —
same call, real Hetzner VPS.

``` python
from fastops import launch

ip = launch(
    src,
    'myapp.example.com',
    envs=envs,
    app_volumes={'./data': '/app/data', './static': '/app/static'},
    extra_dirs=['static', 'onnx-community'],
    server_type='cx22',    # Hetzner instance type
    location='nbg1',       # Nuremberg
)
# Outputs the VPS IP and stores it in FASTOPS_VPS_HOST
```

Pass `tunnel=False` for direct TLS via Caddy (ports 80/443). A proxied
DNS A record is added automatically if `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` is set.

## What Happens Under the Hood

Both [`launch()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch)
and
[`launch_local()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch_local)
orchestrate the full pipeline from a single call:

| Step | What it does |
|:---|:---|
| 🔍 **Detect** | Inspects your repo and generates an optimised [`Dockerfile`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#dockerfile) — FastHTML, Python, Go, Rust, or FastAPI+React |
| 🌐 **Tunnel** | Creates a Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel + CNAME (`tunnel=True`), or a proxied DNS A record for direct TLS (`tunnel=False`) |
| 📦 **Stack** | Builds `docker-compose.yml` + [`Caddyfile`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/proxy.html#caddyfile) — Caddy for TLS, optional CrowdSec bouncer and cloudflared |
| 🖥️ **Provision** | Spins up a Hetzner VPS (or Multipass VM) with Docker via cloud-init — UFW, deploy user, hardened |
| 🚀 **Ship** | rsyncs the app, pushes `.env` via scp, runs `docker compose up -d` |
| 📋 **Record** | Saves the deploy to `~/.config/fastops/deploys.json` for [`backup()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#backup) and [`restore()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#restore) |

## Full Workflow Example

``` python
from pathlib import Path
from fastops import ship, backup, restore, secrets

src    = Path('../myapp')
DOMAIN = 'app.example.com'
envs   = secrets('DATABASE_URL', 'JWT_SECRET', 'S3_BUCKET')
kw     = dict(
    pkgs=['rclone', 'libsqlite3-dev'],
    app_volumes={'./data': '/app/data', './static': '/app/static'},
    extra_dirs=['static'],
)

# First call: provisions VPS + sets up GitHub Actions CI/CD
ip = ship(src, DOMAIN, envs=envs, gh_token=True, **kw)
# → prints: gh secret set VPS_SSH_KEY < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
# → prints: gh secret set VPS_HOST_PROD --body "1.2.3.4"
# From now on, every merge to main auto-deploys via GitHub Actions

# Subsequent calls: just rsyncs + compose up (no re-provisioning)
ship(src)

# Backup data and .env to your laptop
archive = backup('myapp', data_dirs=['data', 'backups'])

# Restore from backup (e.g. after migration)
restore(archive, 'myapp')
```

## CI/CD Auto-Deploy

Pass `gh_token=True` to
[`ship()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#ship) on first
deploy. fastops writes `.fastops/config.json` into your app repo and
pushes a GitHub Actions workflow via the GitHub API:

``` python
# First deploy — provisions VPS and wires up CI/CD in one call
ip = ship(src, 'app.example.com', envs=envs, gh_token=True)
```

The generated `.github/workflows/fastops.yml` runs on every push to
`main`:

1.  **Test** — runs `pytest` (if detected in `pyproject.toml`)
2.  **Deploy** — calls
    [`deploy_safe()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#deploy_safe):
    code snapshot → backup → rsync + compose up
3.  **Health check** — polls the container until healthy or times out
4.  **Rollback** — on failure, restores the pre-deploy snapshot
    automatically
5.  **Record** — commits `deployed_at` + git SHA back to
    `.fastops/config.json`

Add staging later without touching the workflow manually:

``` python
fo_add_env('staging', host='5.6.7.8', domain='staging.app.com', branch='dev')
fo_hook(token=True)  # regenerates + pushes the updated workflow
```

`.fastops/config.json` is git-tracked — deploy history lives in your
repo’s git log.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Modules

fastops is built from composable modules — use only what you need.

| Module | What it does |
|:---|:---|
| [\[`ship`\](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#ship)](nbs/04_ship.ipynb) | End-to-end deploy pipeline — [`ship`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#ship), [`launch`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#launch), [`backup`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#backup), [`restore`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#restore), [`deploy_safe`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#deploy_safe), [`fo_init`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#fo_init), [`fo_hook`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/ship.html#fo_hook) |
| [`core`](nbs/00_core.ipynb) | [`Dockerfile`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#dockerfile) + [`Compose`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#compose) fluent builders, app presets (`fasthtml_app`, [`go_app`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#go_app), [`rust_app`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#rust_app), …) |
| [`proxy`](nbs/02_proxy.ipynb) | Caddy, Cloudflare tunnel, CrowdSec service helpers for [`Compose.svc()`](https://Karthik777.github.io/fastops/core.html#compose.svc) |
| [`cloudflare`](nbs/01_cloudflare.ipynb) | DNS records, Zero Trust tunnels via the Cloudflare API |
| [`vps`](nbs/03_vps.ipynb) | Hetzner provisioning, SSH deploy, rsync, Multipass VM management |

## Podman Support

Set `DOCKR_RUNTIME=podman` to switch all CLI calls to Podman. Generated
Dockerfiles and Compose YAML are runtime-agnostic.

``` sh
export DOCKR_RUNTIME=podman
```
